outcome_text: AI risks and benefits from third-party resources are regularly monitored, and risk controls are applied and documented.
scoring_text: Skill in third-party AI risk monitoring and control, including implementing regular monitoring of AI risks from third-party resources, applying and documenting risk controls for third-party AI components, and maintaining ongoing assessment of third-party AI benefit realization.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in third-party AI risk monitoring and control, including implementing regular monitoring of AI risks from third-party resources, applying and documenting risk controls for third-party AI components, and maintaining ongoing assessment of third-party AI benefit realization.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting AI impact assessments, including risk tiering, harm identification, bias evaluation, human oversight documentation, and residual risk determination.
comments: NIST AI RMF MG-3.1 (MANAGE) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-S-005 is the strongest match (STS: 0.710, cosine: 0.691). Secondary: AG-K-010 (AG, cosine: 0.651), RM-S-001 (RM, cosine: 0.649).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.1/10 (raw 0.710). Shared vocabulary: [ai, risk, skill]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [applying, assessment, benefit, components, control, controls] vs WIDAI terms [assessments, bias, conducting, determination, documentation, evaluation] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.886 (P=0.879, R=0.893). Balanced P/R — the 36-word competency implication and 20-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (89.6%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.691 ≈ STS 0.710 (gap 0.019). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, risk, skill] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 15.4%. Shared tokens: [ai, risk, skill]. NIST AI RMF-only: [applying, assessment, benefit, components, control, controls]. WIDAI-only: [assessments, bias, conducting, determination, documentation, evaluation].
